summary: Add cache-first MSSQL DB context snapshot tooling and Roo workflow integration.
next_action: After PR #12 merges, return to ROO-8 allowed_paths semantics and mechanical gate enforcement planning.
acceptance_criteria: The snapshot tool defaults to offline cache reads and rejects contradictory --refresh --offline usage.
Refresh uses fixed MSSQL catalog queries and does not execute arbitrary SQL or stored procedures.
Default process DB comparison uses shape fingerprints to avoid false drift from full-only reference details.
Refresh reuses cached database detail when table, routine, view, function, trigger modify_date markers, target identity, and collection options are unchanged.
Generated DB context artifacts are ignored by git by default.
Roo workflow skills read .db-context/ first and return needs-db-context when required context is missing or insufficient.
The orchestrator handoff contract recognizes needs-db-context.
Focused Python tests cover no-connect safety, cache reuse, cache non-reuse safety, stale job artifact replacement, redaction, drift comparison, and ignore defaults.
notes: Live MSSQL refresh was not run because DB credentials and explicit refresh approval were not provided.
Mechanical path enforcement remains the next roadmap hardening task.
